native_tests: manual events from another session do not affect current-session decisions
irrelevant in-session events do not count toward a text-match threshold
deterministic eviction keeps the youngest created_at_unix_ms/sequence events
recent_limit selects events by newest created_at_unix_ms and then newest sequence, not insertion order
threshold decisions do not trigger before enough matches and do trigger once enough matches exist
normal /v1/chat/completions requests append session-scoped history and can trigger a later request
concurrent manual writes through the serialized cache all become visible
literal and regex matchers both work
receipts expose scope, count, threshold, matched rule/action, recent_limit, and working-set size in policy_actions
api_surface: POST /v1/policy-cache/events accepts deterministic events with session/run scope and text
GET /v1/policy-cache/recent reads the deterministic in-memory cache
POST /v1/chat/completions feeds chat_request history after evaluating the current request

limitations: The recent-history governor is prototype in-memory state only; restart loses history.
Rules are configured through the existing dynamic governance JSON rather than a typed policy schema.
Regexes are compiled during each evaluation after config validation; acceptable for the bakeoff prototype but not ideal for production hot paths.
Current-session/run isolation depends on caller session_id and/or run_id; requests with neither identifier do not match current-scope history rules.

notes: Changing the session_id on matching manual events causes the out-of-session test to fail.
Removing the post-evaluation chat history append causes the chat_completions_feed_history_for_later_decisions test to fail.
Changing eviction ordering away from created_at_unix_ms then sequence causes the property-style eviction test to fail.
Changing matcher handling from regex to literal-only causes the regex matcher test to fail.
Core behavior is covered by native Rust tests without invoking shared Python, uv, pytest, or held-out evaluators.
No Go or Elixir backend files were touched.
Receipts expose decision trace metadata but do not copy recent-history event text into policy_actions.
Adversarial review found and fixed recent_limit ordering so deterministic created_at_unix_ms controls the recent working set.
